resources.name: Spotify Lambdas
resources.description: All API endpoints for the Song Updater iOS app backend — 5 independent AWS Lambda functions.

resources.name: fetch-new-song
resources.description: Core lambda: fetches Spotify tracks added in the last 24 hours and sends APN push notifications. Triggered by CloudWatch cron and also exposed as a GET HTTP endpoint.

resources.name: Get New Tracks
resources.description: Returns tracks added in the last 24 hours for all playlists watched by the given device. Responds 404 if the device has no watched playlists.

headers.name: device-id
headers.description: The unique device identifier used to look up which playlists to fetch tracks for.

resources.name: spotify-api-token-lambda
resources.description: Spotify OAuth token swap endpoint. Exchanges an authorization code (from the Spotify auth flow) for an access token and refresh token. Requires an API Gateway mapping template to transform the form body — see README.md.

resources.name: Token Swap (Authorization Code → Tokens)
resources.description: Exchanges a Spotify OAuth authorization code for an access_token and refresh_token. The 'code' is obtained after the user completes the Spotify login/auth modal. NOTE: API Gateway must apply the VTL mapping template (see README.md) to transform the form body to { "data": { ... } } before this reaches the lambda.

params.name: code
params.description: The authorization code returned by Spotify after the user completes the OAuth flow.

resources.name: spotify-refresh-token-lambda
resources.description: Spotify OAuth refresh token endpoint. Exchanges a refresh token for a new access token. Requires the same API Gateway mapping template as the token swap lambda.

resources.name: Refresh Token (Refresh Token → New Access Token)
resources.description: Exchanges a Spotify refresh_token for a new access_token. Use this after the access_token from the initial token swap expires. NOTE: API Gateway must apply the VTL mapping template (see README.md) to transform the form body to { "data": { ... } } before this reaches the lambda.

params.name: refresh_token
params.description: The refresh token previously obtained from the token swap endpoint.

resources.name: playlist-table-handler
resources.description: Manages watched playlists in DynamoDB and S3. Uses a reference-counting pattern so that multiple users can watch the same playlist — a playlist is only deleted from DynamoDB/S3 when the last user removes it.

resources.name: Add Playlist
resources.description: Adds a Spotify playlist to the watched list. If the playlist is already being watched by another user, increments its watchCount instead of creating a duplicate entry. Updates both DynamoDB (WatchedPlaylists table) and S3 (playlists.json).

resources.name: Remove Playlist
resources.description: Decrements the watchCount for a playlist. If this is the last user watching it (watchCount reaches 0), deletes the playlist from DynamoDB (WatchedPlaylists), removes all associated track records from SongTrackMapper, and removes the entry from S3 (playlists.json).

resources.name: get-feedback-help
resources.description: Sends user feedback and help messages via AWS SES email. The lambda reads the message from the form body (expects { "body-json": { "message": "..." } } after API Gateway mapping) and emails it to the configured TO_EMAIL address.

resources.name: Send Feedback / Help
resources.description: Sends a feedback or help message via AWS SES. The lambda expects the body to arrive as { "body-json": { "message": "..." } } — API Gateway must apply a mapping template to transform the form-urlencoded body before it reaches the lambda. The path below (/feedback) is a placeholder; update it to match your actual API Gateway route.

params.name: message
params.value: Your feedback or help message here
params.description: The text of the feedback or help request to email via SES.
